heading: Columbia Basin power management
label: 838n Columbia Basin power management
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, and not as part of the Federal Columbia River Transmission System Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Account
children.label: 1 Account
children.text: The term “Account” means the account established by subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 2 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Bonneville Power Administration.

children.heading: Canadian Entitlement
children.label: 3 Canadian Entitlement
children.text: The term “Canadian Entitlement” means the downstream power benefits that Canada is entitled to under Article V of the Treaty Relating to Cooperative Development of the Water Resources of the Columbia River Basin, signed at Washington (15 UST 1555; TIAS 5638).January 17, 1961

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury an account for the purposes of making expenditures to increase bilateral transfers of renewable electric generation between the western United States and Canada.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administrator may make expenditures from the Account for activities to improve electric power system coordination by constructing electric power transmission facilities within the western United States that directly or indirectly facilitate non-carbon emitting electric power transactions between the western United States and Canada.

children.label: i
children.text: ; andSeptember 16, 2024

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which the Canadian entitlement value calculation is terminated or reduced to the actual electric power value to the United States, as determined by the Administrator.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 3 Consultation
children.text: The Administrator shall consult with relevant electric utilities in Canada and appropriate regional transmission planning organizations in considering the construction of transmission activities under this subsection.

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: 4 Authorization
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Account a nonreimburseable amount equal to the aggregated amount of the Canadian Entitlement during the 5-year period preceding .November 15, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: to replace obsolete equipment;

children.label: B
children.text: to maintain reliability and improve efficiency in system performance and operation;

children.label: C
children.text: to create more hydroelectric power capacity in the Pacific Northwest; and

children.label: D
children.text: to ensure the availability of water for irrigation in the event that Columbia River water flows from British Columbia into the United States are insufficient after .September 16, 2024

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated $100,000,000, which shall be nonreimburseable, to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator shall conduct a study considering the potential hydroelectric power value to the Pacific Northwest of increasing the coordination of the operation of hydroelectric and water storage facilities on rivers located in the United States and Canada.

children.label: A
children.text: projected changes to the Pacific Northwest electricity supply;

children.label: B
children.text: potential reductions in greenhouse gas emissions;

children.label: C
children.text: any potential need to increase transmission capacity; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other factor the Administrator considers to be relevant for increasing bilateral coordination.

children.label: A
children.text: the British Columbia or a crown corporation owned by British Columbia;1So in original. The word “the” probably should not appear.1

children.label: B
children.text: the Assistant Secretary;

children.label: C
children.text: the Commissioner of Reclamation; and

children.label: D
children.text: any public utility districts that operate hydroelectric projects on the mainstem of the Columbia River.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated $10,000,000, which shall be nonreimburseable, to carry out this subsection.
